title: Cartridge for analyte preparation for the determination of mycotoxin content in plant material and a device containing the cartridge for determination of mycotoxin content in plant material
abstract: The claimed invention relates to a cartridge for isolation of analytes from solid plant material, and a device containing the cartridge for carrying out the fluorescence analysis of the obtained samples to determine the content of mycotoxins. The cartridge consists of at least four vessels, interlinked together at their lower parts via fluidic channel, and performed with possibility of placement of the plant material sample, affinity adsorbent, extractant and buffer solution inside the vessels. The device consists of the outer housing with a docking stage for a cartridge, fluorescence detection module, means for acquisition/processing of signal from fluorescence detection module, control unit, and power supply unit. The cartridge is equipped with means for fluids pumping and switching of fluids direction, which are connected to the device electrical actuators, and a fluidic channel outlet, adjacent to the adsorbent vessel, connects to an optically transparent spectroscopic cell inside the fluorescence detection module. The claimed invention allows carrying out the rapid and complete process of sample preparation and fulfilling the autonomous analysis of the mycotoxin content in plant material due to the continuous automated process in single medium with minimal possibility of contamination.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. The cartridge for sample preparation and analysis of mycotoxin content in plant material, which consists of interlinked together vessels, characterized in that it comprises at least four vessels interlinked together at their outlet parts via fluidic channel, performed as a tube with opened terminals, equipped in connection of the vessels and the channel with 3- position switches for direction of fluid flow, wherein at least two of the vessels are equipped with means for a fluid pumping, mainly performed as plungers, at least one of said vessels is performed with possibility of loading of a plant material sample and one contains affinity adsorbent, whereas other two of at least four vessels are pre-filled with solvent for extraction and buffer solution and equipped with sealing means. 2. The cartridge according to the claim 1 , characterized in that the mesh filters installed at the outlets of vessels, are equipped with plungers. 3. The cartridge according to the claims 1 or 2, characterized in that the 3 -position switches of the flow direction, performed as 3 -position valves, are enabled for automated control. 4. The cartridge according to the claims 1-3, characterized in that the vessel for loading of a plant material sample contains an additional rotatable mean for agitation. 5. The cartridge according to the claims 1-3, characterized in that the vessel with the affinity adsorbent comprises an area for arranging the sorbent that prevents it from a mechanical contact with a plunger. 6. The cartridge according to any of the claims 1-5, characterized in that the sealing means for the content of vessels, containing the extractant and the buffer solution, are performed as membranes and/or sliding plugs. 7. The cartridge according to any of the claims 1-6, characterized in that it contains an additional vessel with derivatization reagent is 

 equipped with mechanisms for fluid pumping, typically performed as a plunger. 8. The device for analysis of mycotoxin, comprising outer housing, docking stage inside the housing for vessels containing substances for the analyte preparation; fluorescence detection module, which consists of light source, a pair of bandpass optical filters with optically transparent spectroscopic cell between them and light detector; means for acquisition/processing of signal from fluorescence detection module; control unit and power supply unit; and which is characterized in that the docking stage contains installed cartridge according to the claims 1-7, pumping means and 3 -position switches of which are engaged with electrical actuators, wherein fluidic channel outlet, adjacent to the affinity adsorbent vessel, is connected to an optically transparent spectroscopic cell, wherein fluorescence detection module contains as light source the ultraviolet light- emitting diode with bandpass optical filters. 9. The device according to the claim 8, characterized in that electrical actuators of 3-position switches are performed as servomotors and electrical plunger actuators are performed as stepper motors, equipped with means to engage plungers. 10. The device according to the claim 8 or 9, characterized in that the ultraviolet light-emitting diode has dominant emission wavelengths in 350â 380 nm band. 1 1. The device according to any of the claims 8-10, characterized in that the additional agitation mechanism placed into a vessel for a sample loading is performed as a ferromagnetic rotary body and electromagnetic coils outside the vessel.
